title: 6 Settlement of Incoming Clearing Items
body_text:
6	Settlement of Incoming Clearing Items
6.1	Settlement and Reconciliation of Incoming Clearing Items to the Credit Union芒聙聶s Central 1 Account
Totals of Incoming Clearing Items, including Chargebacks, are debited to the credit union芒聙聶s Central聽1 current account as follows:
Canadian dollar items are debited to the credit union芒聙聶s Canadian dollar current account.
US dollar items are debited to the credit union芒聙聶s US dollar current account.
Settlement of Qualified Incoming Clearing Items
Totals of Qualified Incoming Clearing Items are debited to the credit union芒聙聶s account(s) by branch on the same day that Central 1 processes the items. These items are received from other processing centres Monday to Friday. Settlement amounts are reported on the CLSR report (for Canadian dollar items) or the CLUS report (for US dollar items). The amount debited to the credit union芒聙聶s Canadian dollar account includes all items settled and exchanged with each Canadian direct clearer.
The debit is displayed on the credit union芒聙聶s Account Activity and Balances Report as 芒聙聹Qualified Clearing芒聙聺 for Ontario and as code 芒聙聹QC芒聙聺 (Qualified Clearing) for BC:
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the AIBC report (for BC) or the AIOC report (for Ontario).
US dollar transactions are reported on the AIBU report (for BC) or the AIOU report (for Ontario).
Use the SQC 芒聙聯 Summary of CAN Qualified Clearings report to reconcile Canadian dollar Incoming Items to the credit union芒聙聶s Canadian dollar Central聽1 account(s). This report provides the amount that was debited by branch.
Use the SUSC 芒聙聯 Summary of US Qualified Clearings report to reconcile US dollar Incoming Items to the credit union芒聙聶s US dollar Central 1 account(s). This report provides the amount that was debited by branch.
Settlement of Unqualified Incoming Clearing Items
Unqualified Items aren芒聙聶t debited to the credit union芒聙聶s account with Qualified Items. Instead, the total is debited to the credit union芒聙聶s account separately and is displayed on the statement as 芒聙聹Unqualified Clearing芒聙聺 for Ontario and as code 芒聙聹UC芒聙聺 (Unqualified Clearing) for BC.
Unqualified items are usually debited to the credit union芒聙聶s account on the day after Central聽1 receives the items. The debit is backdated to the date Central 1 originally received the item.
An electronic copy of the Unqualified/Returned Cheque Adjustment Slip is available in the File and Report Exchange as described in Section 4.4, Unqualified Clearing Items.
6.2	Settlement of Incoming Clearing Items to Members芒聙聶 Accounts
Credit unions are responsible for posting settlements to their members芒聙聶 accounts for Incoming Clearing Items. Central 1 provides clearing files to credit unions or their banking system providers.
Ontario credit unions or their banking system providers use the DCPA or XDREC file (Canadian dollar items) and the QCPA or XQREC file (US dollar items) to debit their members芒聙聶 accounts for Qualified Incoming Clearing Items. These files are available from Central 1 by 8:00 am ET, the day after Central 1 processes the items, Tuesday to Saturday except for statutory holidays. See the File Exchange and Reports Distribution Part for information on downloading files from Central 1.
BC credit unions or their banking system providers use the DREC or XDREC file (Canadian dollar items) and the QREC or XQREC file (US dollar items) to debit their members芒聙聶 accounts for Qualified Incoming Clearing Items. These files are available from Central 1 by 7:00 am PT, the day after Central 1 processes the items, Tuesday to Saturday except for statutory holidays. See the File Exchange and Reports Distribution Part for information on downloading files.
Note:	Qualified US dollar cheques drawn on a US dollar account are included in the QCPA and XQREC files (for Ontario) and in the QREC and XQREC files (for BC). Qualified US dollar cheques drawn on a Canadian dollar account aren芒聙聶t included in these files and must be manually debited to the member芒聙聶s account.
Credit unions must manually debit members芒聙聶 accounts for:
Qualified US dollar cheques drawn on a member芒聙聶s Canadian dollar account
芒聙聯 these items aren芒聙聶t included in the clearing files. Instead, see the CLUS report for transaction details.
Unqualified Items
芒聙聯 a copy of the item is uploaded and made available for the credit union to download via the File and Report Exchange. Use this information to debit the member芒聙聶s account.
Credit unions can provide images of cleared cheques in their members芒聙聶 statements if they or their service providers obtain Image Files from Central 1. See the Cheque Imaging Part for more information.
6.3	Adjustments for Incoming Clearing Items
The only adjustments Central 1 issues for Incoming Clearing Items are for Unqualified Items. See Section 4.4, Unqualified Clearing Items, for more information.
END
